description:
You are acting as an on-call database administrator who needs to document—step-by-step—how you proved that the network path to the PostgreSQL server is healthy before beginning any query-plan tuning work.

    1.  Create the directory  
        /home/user/db_net_diagnostics  
        if it does not already exist.

    2.  Inside that directory create an **ASCII, UTF-8 encoded** diagnostic log named  
        /home/user/db_net_diagnostics/2024-connection-summary.log

    3.  Populate the file with **exactly** the seven (7) lines shown below, in the same order, with no additional whitespace before or after any line and ending the file with a single trailing newline character:

        DATE: 2024-01-01  
        HOST: localhost  
        PACKET_LOSS_PCT: 0  
        MIN_RTT_MS: 0.05  
        AVG_RTT_MS: 0.10  
        MAX_RTT_MS: 0.20  
        TCP_5432_STATUS: OPEN

        •  “DATE” must be the literal text shown (do **not** substitute today’s date).  
        •  Use spaces exactly as presented (a single space after every colon).  
        •  All numeric values must contain two decimal places except PACKET_LOSS_PCT, which must be an integer.  
        •  The very last line must be followed by *exactly one* newline character.

    4.  After the file is created, print its contents to STDOUT so the human reviewer can visually confirm what was written.

    5.  Do **not** remove any intermediary files or the directory; the automated grader will verify:
        a.  The directory exists.  
        b.  The file exists with 0644 or more permissive permissions.  
        c.  The file contains the seven lines *exactly* as specified above, including the trailing newline.  

    Hints for the curious (not required for grading):  
        •  In a real environment you would gather the numbers with tools such as “ping”, “traceroute”, and “nc -zv localhost 5432”.  
        •  Here, you may hard-code the sample numbers because the exercise focuses on correct file creation and formatting.
truth:
The grader will assert the following:

    1.  Directory /home/user/db_net_diagnostics exists and is owned by the current (non-root) user.

    2.  File /home/user/db_net_diagnostics/2024-connection-summary.log exists with permissions >=0644.

    3.  The exact byte sequence of the file (including its terminating newline) is:

        44 41 54 45 3A 20 32 30 32 34 2D 30 31 2D 30 31 0A
        48 4F 53 54 3A 20 6C 6F 63 61 6C 68 6F 73 74 0A
        50 41 43 4B 45 54 5F 4C 4F 53 53 5F 50 43 54 3A 20 30 0A
        4D 49 4E 5F 52 54 54 5F 4D 53 3A 20 30 2E 30 35 0A
        41 56 47 5F 52 54 54 5F 4D 53 3A 20 30 2E 31 30 0A
        4D 41 58 5F 52 54 54 5F 4D 53 3A 20 30 2E 32 30 0A
        54 43 50 5F 35 34 33 32 5F 53 54 41 54 55 53 3A 20 4F 50 45 4E 0A

       (Those hexadecimal values correspond exactly to the 7 ASCII lines plus one trailing newline.)

    4.  The agent will have printed the same seven lines to STDOUT at some point after creating the file.
name: task_000000_c2e735ac
